methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $178M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Seurat Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $178M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms (including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods yield quantifiable results. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $178M raised as a valuation proxy, implying $303M. The peer-median approach anchors to nine robotics companies at comparable stages, which trade at a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. The peer set includes Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B), 1X Technologies at $10.0B (Series B), and Exotec at $2.0B (Series D).

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $257M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $951M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Seurat's relative competitive position within a heterogeneous peer set spanning early-stage and later-stage robotics ventures. Stage-progression yields a more conservative estimate, while peer medians reflect the capital intensity and scale potential of the sector. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, revenue traction relative to comparables, and path to capital efficiency at scale.
